base.upstream_merge_rule: Change private flyto-engine/flyto-code first, export flyto-warroom CE, pin that CE commit, then apply private overlays for paid editions.

build_steps: Resolve and record the flyto-warroom CE commit SHA.
Run open-core audit against the CE base before overlay.
Apply private edition overlay selected by the signed license tier.
Run capability, entitlement, audit-log, and release-boundary tests.
Write release evidence with CE base commit, overlay profile, image digests, and verification results.
protected_overlay_kinds: Firebase/public rating authority signer and calibration service
AutoFix AI proposal, approval, promotion, and rollback orchestration
build_time_overlay_contract.runtime_source_pull_reason: Enterprise and SaaS builds must be reproducible, signed, offline-capable, and auditable; source is resolved only at package/build time.

closed_source_boundaries: billing, entitlement mutation, commercial gates, and Stripe/offline-license adapters
enterprise SSO/SAML/SCIM, legal hold, airgap installers, deployment edition internals
darkweb, stealer-log, proprietary phishing-feed credentials/data, commercial threat-intel, and proprietary correlation datasets
cloud/container/runtime live remediation orchestration and customer connector credentials
Flyto2 Cloud Enterprise Bridge services, entitlement signer, managed job execution plane, and hosted SaaS control plane
AutoFix promotion, approval, rollback orchestration, and commercial AI proposal workflows
hosted SaaS-only frontend configuration, private preview credentials, and enterprise image publishing metadata

private_boundaries: private unified cockpit and enterprise routes
authoritative scoring and proprietary correlation presentation
managed provider credentials and paid connector UX
live remediation approval rollback and commercial automation
frontend_source_boundary.reason: Generated CE source is exported only from the dedicated src-ce tree; private src-next is neither copied nor pruned, preserving a reviewable physical frontend boundary.

merge_contracts: Source-available CE exports are generated artifacts; do not hand-edit generated community trees.
Every package must declare source repo, license, include patterns, protected paths, and deny path patterns.
Exporter fails closed on missing include patterns, missing required paths, protected path inclusion, and denied content markers.
CE is the public upstream base; Enterprise, SaaS, on-prem, and airgap editions are build-time overlays, not forks.
Community packages may call back into enterprise only through documented contracts, not private imports.
